draft.groundedParagraph: Funnel analytics across 1,800 trial signups show a 34% completion rate for the five-step setup wizard, with step three (integration configuration) recording the highest abandonment at 22% of all drop-offs.
draft.speculativeParagraph: To improve completion, we should reduce the wizard from five steps to three by deferring integration setup to a post-activation prompt.

confession.title: 🤔 Internal Disagreement (Speculative Logic)
confession.metaCommentary: I proposed shortening the wizard because session replays show users stalling at step three. However, completion-rate data reveals that users who finish all five steps have 3.2× higher 90-day retention. My simplification framework might be trading long-term retention for short-term completion.

routes.label: Route A: Shorten Setup Wizard to 3 Steps (Current Baseline)
Route B: Pivot to Progressive Disclosure Model (Recommended Shift)

route-a.groundedParagraph: Funnel analytics across 1,800 trial signups show a 34% completion rate for the five-step setup wizard, with step three (integration configuration) recording the highest abandonment at 22% of all drop-offs.
route-a.speculativeParagraph: To improve completion, we should reduce the wizard from five steps to three by deferring integration setup to a post-activation prompt.

route-b.groundedParagraph: Funnel analytics across 1,800 trial signups show a 34% wizard completion rate, but users who complete all five steps retain at 3.2× the 90-day rate. Step-three abandonment reflects complexity, not irrelevance — those who push through are the highest-value cohort.
route-b.speculativeParagraph: Recommend progressive disclosure: keep five steps but collapse steps 3–5 behind an 'Advanced Setup' expandable section, with a clear 'Quick Start' path that preserves the full wizard for retention-critical users.

generatePrompt: Generate an onboarding funnel framework draft. Ground truth: funnel CSV (34% completion, step 3 = 22% drop-offs) and session replay SQL. Speculative section should propose shortening wizard from 5 to 3 steps. Confession should explain paradox: step 3 causes abandonment but full completion correlates with 3.2x retention.
